item.name: developerMetadata

description.content: Operations on developer metadata

item.name: Google Sheets Get Developer Metadata

description.content: Returns the developer metadata with the specified ID. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID and the developer metadata's unique metadataId.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve or modify.
(Required) The ID of the developer metadata to retrieve.

response.name: Successful response containing the developer metadata.

item.name: Spreadsheets

description.content: Operations on spreadsheet resources

item.name: Google Sheets Create a Spreadsheet

description.content: Creates a new spreadsheet, optionally with initial properties, sheets, and data.

response.name: Successful response containing the newly created spreadsheet.

item.name: Google Sheets Get a Spreadsheet

description.content: Returns the spreadsheet at the given ID. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID. By default, data within grids is not returned. You can include grid data in one of two ways using the fields URL parameter or the includeGridData query parameter.

query.description: The ranges to retrieve from the spreadsheet.
The ranges to retrieve from the spreadsheet.
True if grid data should be returned. This parameter is ignored if a field mask was set in the request.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve or modify.

response.name: Successful response containing the spreadsheet.

item.name: Google Sheets Batch Update a Spreadsheet

request.name: Google Sheets Batch Update a Spreadsheet

description.content: Applies one or more updates to the spreadsheet. Each request is validated before being applied. If any request is not valid, the entire request will fail and nothing will be applied.

response.name: Successful response containing the batch update results.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Google Sheets Get a Spreadsheet by Data Filter

request.name: Google Sheets Get a Spreadsheet by Data Filter

description.content: Returns the spreadsheet at the given ID. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID. This method differs from getSpreadsheet in that it allows selecting which subsets of spreadsheet data to return by specifying a dataFilters parameter.

response.name: Successful response containing the spreadsheet.

item.name: Values

description.content: Operations on spreadsheet cell values

item.name: Google Sheets Get Values From a Range

request.name: Google Sheets Get Values From a Range

description.content: Returns a range of values from a spreadsheet. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID and a range.

query.description: The major dimension that results should use.
How values should be represented in the output.
How dates, times, and durations should be represented in the output.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve or modify.
(Required) The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the range to read or modify. For example, Sheet1!A1:D5 or Sheet1!R1C1:R5C4.

response.name: Successful response containing the requested values.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve or modify.
(Required) The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the range to read or modify. For example, Sheet1!A1:D5 or Sheet1!R1C1:R5C4.

item.name: Google Sheets Update Values in a Range

request.name: Google Sheets Update Values in a Range

description.content: Sets values in a range of a spreadsheet. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID, range, and a valueInputOption.

query.description: (Required) How the input data should be interpreted.
Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated.
Determines how values in the response should be rendered.
Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve or modify.
(Required) The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the range to read or modify. For example, Sheet1!A1:D5 or Sheet1!R1C1:R5C4.

response.name: Successful response containing the update result.

item.name: Google Sheets Append Values to a Range

request.name: Google Sheets Append Values to a Range

description.content: Appends values to a spreadsheet. The input range is used to search for existing data and find a table within that range. Values will be appended to the next row of the table, starting with the first column of the table.

query.description: (Required) How the input data should be interpreted.
How the input data should be inserted.
Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were appended.
Determines how values in the response should be rendered.
Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve or modify.

response.name: Successful response containing the append result.

item.name: Google Sheets Clear Values From a Range

request.name: Google Sheets Clear Values From a Range

description.content: Clears values from a spreadsheet. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID and range. Only values are cleared; all other properties of the cell (such as formatting and data validation) are kept.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve or modify.

response.name: Successful response containing the cleared range.

The Google Sheets API lets you read, write, and format data in Google Sheets. It supports creating spreadsheets, reading and writing cell values, updating formatting, managing sheets within a spreadsheet, and working with developer metadata.

Contact Support:
 Name: Google Workspace Developer Support
